title: Rustic Aerated Venezuelan Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A unique and flavorful Venezuelan-inspired dish featuring aerated buckwheat pilaf, perfect for adventurous eaters.
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery, and sauté until the vegetables are tender.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the buckwheat flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the chicken broth, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add the dried aji amarillo pepper, cumin, and black pepper to the pilaf, and stir to combine.
Using a fork, aerate the pilaf by gently scratching the surface of the mixture.
Continue to simmer the pilaf for another 5-10 minutes, or until it has reached the desired consistency.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro if desired.
